ion-button-style md button button-round button-solid ion-activatable ion-focusable hydrated

reviewSummary.summary: The draft captures the primary login controls but contains duplicate password elements, weak semantic naming, missing navigation and validation elements, and includes decorative noise that should not be part of the canonical page model.

issues.message: Two separate password-related elements represent the same functional password input control.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep only the native actionable password textbox with locator id=ion-input-1 and remove the wrapper ion-input element with id=password.

issues.message: The element name 'password' is too generic and does not clearly describe the control type.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename the element to 'password_textbox' for downstream automation clarity and consistency.

issues.message: The background content container is decorative layout structure and not useful for workflow automation.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the background_content element from the approved page model.

issues.message: The Login page workflow explicitly requires Back and Home navigation controls, but neither control exists in the draft page elements.
issues.suggested_fix: Add dedicated back_button and home_button elements using stable actionable locators.

issues.message: The workflow requires validation of the generic Failed authentication message, but no authentication error or failed message element exists in the page model.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a reusable generic_failed_authentication_message element with a stable visible-text or message-container locator.

issues.message: The ion-input wrapper element is classified as a textbox even though it behaves as a container/wrapper around the actual native input.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the wrapper element or reclassify it as a container if retained for page-state validation.

issues.message: The locator relies entirely on visible text and may become brittle if UI wording changes.
issues.suggested_fix: Prefer a stable id, data-testid, aria-label, or semantic attribute if available in the DOM.

issues.message: The workflow requires validation of authenticated user identity display and authenticated-only feature visibility after successful login, but no destination-state verification elements are represented.
issues.suggested_fix: Add authenticated user identity and authenticated feature area elements in the upstream Home page model for reusable authenticated-state validation.

approved_elements.name: password_textbox
approved_elements.description: Password input field with masked entry behavior.
